questions.question: Is the signed-card cache genuinely keyed by nickname and rebuilt only when the nickname changes, or does it silently rebuild — and round-trip to a remote NIP-46 signer — on every share-modal open regardless of whether the nickname actually changed?

Does settings.tsx build the card via encodeCard using activeEventSignerOverride.current ?? createPrivateKeySigner(privateKeyHex) (the existing signer-selection precedent), and does NpubQrModal's display mode consume only the SignedCardPayload seam (payload/shareUrl) produced by S1, without NpubQrModal.tsx itself invoking a signer or the codec directly?

Could `NpubQrModal / share` be deleted and rewritten without modifying any file outside its `dependencies_allowed` list? If not, which cross-module dependencies exist and do they flow through declared seam contracts in architecture.md?

Are there adapter-level unit tests for card production in local-key, NIP-07 (stubbed sign), and NIP-46 (stubbed sign) modes, each asserting the produced card verifies via real getEventHash/verifyEvent (not a stubbed verify), following the vi.mock nostr-tools/pure pattern precedent (callSignaling.test.ts) for stubbing only the remote sign call, never the crypto primitives?

Does the share action produce a QR encoding the full onboarding URL (https://few.chat/add#c=<payload>) at ECC-L instead of the bare npub at ECC-M, matching spec.md's stated share behavior, with the no-nickname-set case producing an unsigned card equivalent to sharing a bare npub?

Does the share-card caching strategy avoid holding the user's private key material, or an unnecessary long-lived remote-signer reference, in component state beyond what createPrivateKeySigner/activeEventSignerOverride already require, and is the cache correctly invalidated on both a nickname change and a signer-mode change (e.g. switching from local key to NIP-46 mid-session)?

Does the 'Share contact card' action produce a copy-able card link and a scannable QR encoding the full onboarding URL (not the bare payload), built from the current user's signed card via the active signer, verifying (AC-SIG-1) in local, NIP-07, and NIP-46 modes via adapter-level unit tests (real getEventHash, stubbed remote sign) plus a local-mode e2e, with the signed card cached and rebuilt only on nickname change? Show the production settings.tsx/NpubQrModal.tsx code, the three adapter-mode unit tests plus the e2e test reference, and confirm the unit tests assert real verification rather than stubbing verifyEvent itself.

settings.tsx's handleOpenShareCard only calls ownQrDisclosure.onOpen() AFTER getOwnShareCard resolves (never opens the modal first and lets it render a stale/blank QR while the signer round-trip is pending). Does this correctly avoid the alternative of passing an undefined shareUrl into an already-open modal, and does the NpubQrButton's isLoading={shareCardLoading} give the user visible feedback during a slow NIP-46 round trip rather than an unexplained delay before the modal appears?

The shareCard.test.ts case titled 'rejects a signer whose pubkey does not match the card subject' exercises encodeCard's pre-sign pubkey-mismatch guard, not decode-time verifyEvent rejection (a distinct failure mode from AC-SIG-3's post-sign tampering). Is this honestly scoped by its in-test comment, and does the epic's existing AC-SIG-2/AC-SIG-3 coverage in contactCard.test.ts (S1) already cover the decode-time tamper case, so this story does not need to duplicate it?

NpubQrModal's display mode dropped the standalone muted <Text>{npub}</Text> line (previously shown above the Code block) in favour of a single Code block driven by displayValue (shareUrl when present, else npub unchanged). Since settings.tsx is confirmed (via grep) to be the only display-mode caller and no e2e test asserts on the removed line, is this an acceptable minor visual simplification rather than a scope violation, and should a future display-mode caller with different UX needs get its own prop rather than reintroducing this line unconditionally?

getOwnShareCard's lazy getSignEvent is only invoked after shouldRebuildShareCard returns true (verified by the 'sign called exactly once across two opens' unit test). Does this ordering hold in production settings.tsx too -- i.e. does handleOpenShareCard never resolve/construct a signer object before calling getOwnShareCard, so a cache hit in NIP-46 mode truly never touches signerAdapter at all (not even to construct an unused signer instance)?

Gate-remediation fix (review severity 6, impl_bug): ShareCardCacheKey now includes pubkeyHex alongside nickname/signerMode, and shouldRebuildShareCard compares all three. settings.tsx sources pubkeyHex directly from useNostrIdentity() rather than re-deriving it from npub. Does this close the mid-session identity-restore bug -- sharing as identity A then restoring identity B via replaceIdentity's in-place setIdentity() (no page reload) with the same (nickname, signerMode) as A -- for every restore path, including the mnemonic/seed-phrase restore flow and any future caller of replaceIdentity, or only for the specific reproduction scenario the regression tests exercise (empty-nickname/local-signer, and same-nonempty-nickname/local-signer)? Is there any restore path where the identity context's pubkeyHex getter could still observe the previous identity's value at the moment handleOpenShareCard reads it (a stale-closure risk distinct from the cache-key bug itself)?
